lenses.label: All
lenses.description: Everything in the mathematics and physics corpus.

lenses.label: Dynamical Systems & Ergodic Theory
lenses.description: Topological and symbolic dynamics, hyperbolicity and structural stability, the ergodic theorems, mixing and spectral theory, entropy, and smooth ergodic theory. Prerequisites are pulled in automatically so the result is always a complete, learnable path.

lenses.label: Probability & Stochastics
lenses.description: Measure-theoretic probability, limit theorems, martingales, Markov chains, stochastic calculus, large deviations, and random matrices. Prerequisites are pulled in automatically so the result is always a complete, learnable path.

lenses.label: Pure Mathematics
lenses.description: Mathematics on its own terms — geometry, algebra, analysis, number theory, and the rest — with the physics chapters hidden. Prerequisites are pulled in automatically so the result is always a complete, learnable path.

lenses.label: Theoretical Physics
lenses.description: The mathematics a theoretical physicist needs — the Fast Track curriculum plus the physics it builds toward. As new pure-math spines are added that a physicist does not need, this lens keeps showing only the physics-relevant path.
